ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Has the person ever shown self-injurious behavior? (e.g. head banging, head-punching or slapping, removing hair, self-scratching, body hitting, eye poking or pressing).
Has the person shown self-injurious behavior in the last month? (e.g. head banging, head-punching or slapping, removing hair, self-scratching, body hitting, eye poking or pressing).

Place a tick next to the items in the following list of self-injurious behaviors, which the person displays in a repetitive manner (repeats the same movement/ behavior twice or more in succession): Hits self with body part (e.g., slaps head or face)

Place a tick next to the items in the following list of self-injurious behaviors, which the person displays in a repetitive manner (repeats the same movement/ behavior twice or more in succession): Hits self against surface or object (e.g., bands head on floor or table)

Place a tick next to the items in the following list of self-injurious behaviors, which the person displays in a repetitive manner (repeats the same movement/ behavior twice or more in succession): Hits self with object

Place a tick next to the items in the following list of self-injurious behaviors, which the person displays in a repetitive manner (repeats the same movement/ behavior twice or more in succession): Bites self (e.g., bites hand on wrist or arm)

Place a tick next to the items in the following list of self-injurious behaviors, which the person displays in a repetitive manner (repeats the same movement/ behavior twice or more in succession): Pulls (e.g., pulls hair or skin)

Place a tick next to the items in the following list of self-injurious behaviors, which the person displays in a repetitive manner (repeats the same movement/ behavior twice or more in succession): Rubs or scratches self (e.g., rub marks on arm or leg)

Place a tick next to the items in the following list of self-injurious behaviors, which the person displays in a repetitive manner (repeats the same movement/ behavior twice or more in succession): Inserts finger or objects (e.g., eye poking)

Place a tick next to the items in the following list of self-injurious behaviors, which the person displays in a repetitive manner (repeats the same movement/ behavior twice or more in succession): Other form of self-injury
If other form of self-injury is checked, please specify here.
In the last month, for how long did the episode or burst of this self-injurious behavior last? (Please select one option)
Notes: 1=Less than a minute; 2=Less than 5 minutes; 3=Less than 15 minutes; 4=Less than an hour; 5=More than an hour

ElementDescription: In the last month as a result of this self-injurious behavior, has physical contact or prevention or restraint by others been necessary (e.g., blocking, taking objects from an individual, temporary restraint of an arm)? (Please select one option)
Notes: 0=Never; 1=At least once a month; 2=At least once a week; 3=At least once a day; 4=At least once an hour

ElementDescription: Think about how often this self-injurious behavior occurred in the last month. If there was no change and you watched the person now, then would you definitely see the behavior:
Notes: 1=By this time next month; 2=By this time next week; 3=By this time tomorrow; 4=In the next hour; 5=In the next 15 minutes
